element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Used to collect power-ups from scrolls and defeat enemies
Screen-clearing power for crowd control; collected via scrolls
Losing a life if time expires before level completion; constant pressure
No health meter; single touch = life lost
Alternative movement mode requiring different timing
Vertical navigation; introduces aerial enemy encounters
Vertical scaling puzzle; uses early iteration Noid sprite variant
Culminates progression with special mechanics distinct from standard levels

typical_relief_triggers: Power scroll collection enabling screen-clear special attack
Extra lives awarded per 20,000 points scored
Transitioning to skateboard or autogyro for environmental change
typical_tension_triggers: Enemy encounters requiring precise yo-yo timing
Tight platforming sections without safe space
Vertical traversal sequences (building climb, autogyro sections)
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Rapid; constant time pressure balanced by frequent power-up opportunities and alternating traversal modes maintaining engagement across diverse stage themes

spatial_grammar.optional_content_signal: Large scrolls containing magic points and rare power-ups require weapon activation to open; incentivizes exploration and resource seeking

difficulty_curve.early_game: Introduces basic jumping and yo-yo mechanics on accessible pier environment; establishes time limit pressure gradually
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Expands traversal options (skateboard); increases enemy density and encounter complexity; maintains time pressure
difficulty_curve.late_game: Introduces vertical challenges (autogyro flight, building climb at Stage 13); Stage 13 features different Noid sprite iteration suggesting intentional visual difficulty escalation; culminates in boss pizza-eating contests
notable_spikes: Stage 13 vertical building climb with early-iteration sprite variant
Autogyro flight sequences introducing aerial enemy patterns
Final boss encounters shifting from standard platforming to special contest mechanics

notable_level_patterns.pattern_name: Linear horizontal progression with vertical pickups
notable_level_patterns.description: Early stages move player left-to-right with optional scrolls positioned above/below primary path; yo-yo reach determines resource access
Mid-game introduces skateboard and autogyro sections replacing standard platforming, forcing mechanic adaptation while maintaining core time pressure
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Skateboard and autogyro stages (unspecified stage numbers)

notable_level_patterns.description: Building climb requires upward navigation with heightened enemy threat; Stage 13 uses visually distinct early-iteration sprite, possibly signaling narrative or difficulty milestone
Terminal stage sections shift from platforming to specialized contest (pizza-eating); rewards accumulated points system and resource management

sources_used: tcrf - Yo! Noid (The Cutting Room Floor)
